channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Homeland Security suspends TSA PreCheck and Global Entry airport security programs
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security is suspending the TSA PreCheck and Global Entry airport security programs as a partial government shutdown co...

item.title: Only a fraction of House seats are competitive. Redistricting is driving that lower
item.description: Primary voters in a small number of districts play an outsized role in deciding who wins Congress. The Trump-initiated mid-decade redistricting is dri...

item.title: Crews recover bodies of all 9 skiers killed in California avalanche
item.description: The bodies of all the skiers who died in Tuesday's avalanche near Lake Tahoe have been recovered, authorities said Saturday. Intense winter storm cond...

item.title: How the seat position in a murdered teen's car linked to her killer
item.description: Sade Robinson, 19, disappeared after a first date. Milwaukee investigators say clues in her car pointed to her assailant.

item.title: Sade Robinson and The Secret Beach
item.description: A teenage girl goes missing. Will evidence on a secluded beach reveal what happened? "48 Hours" correspondent Anne-Marie Green reports.

item.title: Blizzard warnings send East Coast scrambling to prepare for heavy snow and strong winds
"48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: How football is helping girls fight against forced marriage
item.description: Two sisters in an Indian village talk about how football has changed their lives and helped them push back against child marriage.

item.title: Cati Blauvelt: Death of a Soldier's Wife
item.description: A soldier goes on the run after his wife is murdered. With him, his 17-year-old girlfriend. What does she know? "48 Hours" correspondent Peter Van San...

item.title: Pakistan launches deadly strikes on Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistan says the strikes were retaliation for recent suicide bombings in the country.

item.title: See the Olympics schedule for the 2026 Milano Cortina Games
item.description: This is what you need to know about when the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics start and end, along with key games and events on the schedule.

item.title: More than 1,500 Venezuelan political prisoners apply for amnesty
item.description: The announcement by the head of Venezuela's National Assembly comes amid US pressure following the capture of ex-President NicolÃ¡s Maduro.

item.title: Willie ColÃ³n, trombonist who pioneered salsa music, dies aged 75
item.description: His career spanned 60 years and dozens of albums and had been named among the most influential Latino artists of all time.

item.title: Final missing Lake Tahoe skier found dead 5 days after avalanche
item.description: Authorities released the names of the six women and three guides who died in the worst avalanche in state history.

item.title: FCC calls for more 'patriotic, pro-America' programming in run-up to 250th anniversary
item.description: The "Pledge America Campaign" urges broadcasters to focus on programming that highlights "the historic accomplishments of this great nation from our f...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II lunar mission may not launch in March after all
item.description: NASA says an "interrupted flow" of helium to the rocket system could require a rollback to the Vehicle Assembly Building. If it happens, NASA says the...

item.title: Crews recover bodies of 9 backcountry skiers days after California avalanche
item.description: Authorities say crews have recovered the bodies of nine backcountry skiers who were killed in a California avalanche.

item.title: ICEâs purchases for big detention centers are marked by secrecy, frustrating towns
